title: Mateo Mori
text:
Welcome!
Hello, I am an environmental science data science and statistics student interested in visualizing the world through R. I made this website to store my professional and academic experiences along with photos and my resume/CV all in one place.
I am Venezuelan and Chilean and San Diego is where I call home. I am currently working at UCSB’s Arts & Lectures events organization as a Hospitality Manager. I am also working with Fatiq Nadeem at the Bren School of Environmental Science and Management on fire data in India through Planet Scope satellite imagery and qGIS. I am always looking for new experiences in Environmental Science and opportunities where I can grow as a person in society.


Education
Environmental Studies B.S. (2027) | University of California, Santa Barbara
High Tech High School (2023)

title: Relevant Coursework
text:
We visited various sites along the coast that had restoration projects and interesting physical and social geographic features.
Research project on the effects of seasonal changes in kelp forest density on beach wrack and beach productivity.
Performed native plant identification and did vegetation transect surveys.
Taught by Ian Walker




Doing vegetation cover studies on dune restoration project at Santa Monica Beach.

title: About Me
text: If I have some free time, I am probably playing water polo, scuba diving, surfing, backpacking, or planning my next trip.

title: About Me
section: A few things I enjoy doing
text:
A few things I enjoy doing






Backpacking the Pacific Crest Trail with my mom near San Diego. This was my first ever backpacking experience and getting a taste of the great outdoors has only made me want to do it more. Fall 2025







Playing for UCSB Men’s Water Polo Club is a blast, especially when I can start a game next to my roomate Chris. Winter 2026








Devereux, Fall 2025
Devereux, Fall 2025




Tidepooling at Devereux Beach in Isla Vista is one of my favorite ways to experience nature. Doing it at night takes things to the next level, and going with my Aquatic Biology roomates turned this into such a fun learning opportunity. Here I spotted a California Two-spot Octopus (Octopus bimaculoides) immediately after telling my roomates that the one thing I wanted to find that night was an octopus. (I then proceeded to find another octopus!)







Summer, 2025




Catching non-native Peacock Bass (Cichla ocellaris) with my Venezuelan cousins in a backyard freshwater river in Miami, Florida. Visiting my cousins in Florida is always so much fun, and we love going to our hidden little spots that we have been going to for years.
